title: Fix macOS release packaging and add macOS CI coverage
action: Per D8, in release.yml's build job Package step (the shared step copying config.example.json/install.sh/README.md and packaging/herdr-go.service into every matrix entry's archive today), make the packaging/herdr-go.service copy conditional so the aarch64-apple-darwin entry copies packaging/herdr-go.plist (from cross-platform-install-2) instead, while both Linux entries keep copying packaging/herdr-go.service exactly as before - use a matrix.target-conditional shell branch inline in that step (mirroring how the Build binary step already branches on matrix.cross), not a separate job (unlike windows-release-matrix's Windows entry, install.sh legitimately belongs in the macOS archive too, so this stays inside the shared build job). Per D9, add a new top-level job to ci.yml named macos (or similar), runs-on: macos-14 (pinned, matching release.yml's existing pin - never macos-latest), running the same cargo fmt --all --check / cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings / cargo test sequence the existing rust job runs on ubuntu-latest (mirror that job's steps: actions/checkout, dtolnay/rust-toolchain with rustfmt+clippy components, Swatinem/rust-cache).

truths: The macOS release archive contains packaging/herdr-go.plist and does NOT contain packaging/herdr-go.service
Both Linux release archives still contain packaging/herdr-go.service exactly as before, unchanged
ci.yml has a new job pinned to macos-14 running fmt/clippy/test
The Linux and Windows CI jobs in ci.yml are unchanged

artifacts.substantive: valid YAML, Package step has a real OS-conditional branch for the service/plist file, no TODO
valid YAML, new macos-14 job with real fmt/clippy/test steps

key_links: The macOS archive's plist file is the exact packaging/herdr-go.plist created in cross-platform-install-2, not a new inline copy
prohibitions: Do not modify the Linux matrix entries' Package step content
Do not modify the existing rust/web/windows-server-2022 jobs in ci.yml
Do not use macos-latest or any floating runner alias - pin macos-14 explicitly

verify: python3 -c "import yaml; d=yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/release.yml')); k='bu'+'ild'; j=d['jobs'][k]; steps_text=str(j); assert 'herdr-go.plist' in steps_text, 'missing plist reference'; ci=yaml.safe_load(open('.github/workflows/ci.yml')); cijobs=ci.get('jobs',{}); macjob=[x for x in cijobs.values() if 'macos-14' in str(x.get('runs-on',''))]; assert macjob, 'no macos-14 job found'; print('OK')"

trace.outcome: release.yml Package step now OS-conditionally copies packaging/herdr-go.plist for the aarch64-apple-darwin entry (herdr-go.service for both Linux entries, unchanged); ci.yml gained a pinned macos-14 job (fmt/clippy/test)
